close_reason: Merged in PR #15: stable #intake anchor on the booking block + explicit hero scroll (next/link doesn't jump for same-page hashes).
description: Every 'Book a Consultation' CTA targets #intake, but BlockRenderer derived section ids by slugifying the block headline, so the booking block rendered as #start-with-a-brain-health-assessment-and-consultation. The anchor never existed: Header.handleConsultationClick's getElementById('intake') returned null and preventDefault'd into a no-op. Additionally the hero CTA goes through @pbh/ui Button -> next/link, which updates the hash for a same-page anchor without performing the jump.
title: Hero/header/footer CTAs don't scroll to the booking form

close_reason: StickyActions pins the submit button in the modal's scroll area with a blurred overlay; bottom padding moved off the modal body so the bar pins flush and does not jump at the end of scroll.
description: On the long modal steps (details, consent) the submit button sat below the fold and could be clipped off the viewport entirely. Pin it to the bottom of the modal's scroll area with a blurred overlay so content scrolls under it, keeping the pinned header and the action always visible.
title: Booking modal: pin the submit button so it stays visible

close_reason: Root cause was @pbh/booking never registered as a Tailwind @source, so space-y-6 was never generated. Registered it and split the row gap (16px) from the CTA gap (24px) per Figma 751:1482.
description: SignupForm's fieldset uses a single space-y-6 (24px), which applies uniformly between the name row, the email field, and the submit button. Figma 751:1482 wants two values: form-field/row-gap = 16px between field rows, and form-card/gap = 24px before the CTA. Split the two so the rows sit at 16px.
title: Booking signup form: field-row gap doesn't match Figma

description: Replace the naive EMAIL_RE in signup with a robust syntactic validator + normalization (trim/lowercase) in a funnel-local lib/email.ts. Wire into signup and login. Funnel-only for now.
title: Stronger email validation in the funnel

description: Production landing page from DE2 on the scaffold.
title: Build: Landing page

description: Account + versioned consent capture; writes to schema.
notes:
Refresh/resume gap (follow-up, tied to auth — pbh-bws.18/FT18 Clerk session):

Consent step shipped on branch tuily/pbh-bws.4 (commit e5341f8) as a single-page client stepper (StepFlow.tsx). Step position + userId live in CLIENT MEMORY only. On a mid-funnel page refresh the client resets to step 1 (signup); since the account is already persisted server-side, re-submitting the same email hits the users_email_unique guard -> "email already registered" dead end, stranding the user before consent.

Docs do NOT mandate surviving an in-flow refresh, but the funnel is built around server-persisted checkpoints + email-based recovery (FT16 abandonment/resume emails), so this is a real drop-off gap. Decision for now: ship as-is (option 1). Proper fix rides with auth/session: on /get-started, derive the resume step server-side from existing state (account exists? consent rows exist? payment?) instead of client memory. Cheap stopgap if needed sooner: persist {stepIndex,userId} to sessionStorage/cookie (client-trusted, same caveat as the hidden userId field).

Also note: userId is passed to the consent server action via a client-trusted hidden field — replace with session-derived user once auth lands.
title: Build: Account creation + consent capture

Force-closed ahead of open ops/spec/design/env deps (.3 BAA, .5, .9, .17, .39) — those remain open on their own tracks as real launch gates.

description:
apps/funnel has NO test runner today (only apps/marketing has vitest, wired solely for Storybook). Add vitest (node/happy-dom env) so the funnel's server logic can be unit-tested.

Scope:
- Add vitest dev dep to apps/funnel (reuse the version already in the repo: vitest ^4.1.3, per apps/marketing).
- Add apps/funnel/vitest.config.ts (node environment; happy-dom only if component tests are later needed).
- Add a "test": "vitest run" script to apps/funnel/package.json.
- Add a 'test' task to the root turbo.json pipeline (currently only build/dev/start/lint/typecheck).

No app-behavior change. This is reusable harness infrastructure: it unblocks the Linus API integration tests and the existing Stripe/webhook chaos test (pbh-bws.30).
title: Bootstrap vitest test harness in apps/funnel

description:
The Linus API integration is the funnel's highest-stakes external seam (register subject + enroll in cognitive assessments post-payment) and is currently UNTESTED. Add tests covering apps/funnel/src/lib/linus/* and apps/funnel/src/app/assessments/register-and-enroll.ts.

Depends on pbh-bws.42 (vitest harness must exist first).

Two layers:

1. Pure-unit (no network/DB):
   - extractReportData (client.ts): base64 reportData out of the array report shape.
   - buildRegisterInput + MissingDateOfBirthError (build-register-input.ts).
   - toSexAssignedAtBirth mapping + OTHER default.
   - getCampaigns env switch (campaigns.ts) via VERCEL_ENV (sandbox vs prod ids).
   - getLinusConfig missing-var error message (env.ts).

2. Mocked HTTP/DB (higher value):
   - client.ts: mock fetch to cover getAccessToken token caching/refresh (60s safety margin), bearer + User-Agent headers, and LinusApiError {status, body} thrown on non-2xx.
   - register-and-enroll.ts concurrency/idempotency logic (mock the DB layer):
     - ensureRegistered claim-win / deferred / error outcomes and retryOnContention behavior (webhook throws->500->Stripe redelivers vs client shows success).
     - storeParticipantId unique-violation (23505 / PgErrorCode.UniqueViolation) fallback to already-stored id.
     - resolveEnrollments 4-step ladder, esp. the invariant: NEVER re-POST an active/started enrollment (Linus enrollSubject mints a new one for 'started', orphaning the report).

Note: no HTTP-mocking pattern exists in the repo yet - establish one (vitest vi.mock / vi.stubGlobal('fetch', ...)); it becomes the template for future funnel integration tests.
title: Linus API integration tests (unit + mocked HTTP/DB)

description:
Implement the 5 Figma booking/assessment StepFlow designs as a MODAL sequence on the marketing site, full end-to-end (real account, consent, Stripe payment, DB writes, Linus enrollment).

Figma: https://www.figma.com/design/SppKdzsaH6rQ14u90UpNSq/Primary-Brain-Health

DESIGN -> STEP MAPPING (all 5 already exist as funnel DEMO code, being re-homed to marketing):
- 360-2502  Landing booking section 'Start With a Brain Health assessment & Consultation' (First/Last/Email + Includes checklist + $149 / HSA-FSA note). Ref: apps/funnel .../signup/SignupForm.tsx + marketing IntakeForm block.
- 360-2854  Modal 1 'Welcome, {name}' details (DOB, gender, ZIP, phone, education, concerns). Ref: apps/funnel .../details/DetailsForm.tsx.
- 466-245 / 360-3214  Modal 2 'Almost there!' terms; scroll-to-enable -> 'Continue With Payment'. Ref: apps/funnel .../consent/ConsentForm.tsx.
- 360-3226  Modal 3 'Payment' — Stripe Embedded Checkout. Ref: apps/funnel .../payment/PaymentStep.tsx.

DECISIONS (from user):
- Flow lives ENTIRELY on the marketing site, presented as a MODAL sequence.
- Funnel /get-started stepper was DEMO scaffolding — not the production surface (retire it).
- Scope = full end-to-end.

BACKEND RECOMMENDATION — consolidate server logic into marketing via shared workspace packages (not copy-paste, not cross-app API):
- @pbh/db       Drizzle schema + migrations + Neon client (from apps/funnel/src/db)
- @pbh/payments Stripe server helpers + Checkout Session + webhook (from apps/funnel/src/lib/stripe)
- @pbh/linus    enrollment client (from apps/funnel/src/lib/linus)
- Re-home the 4 step server actions (createAccount, completeProfile, recordConsent, createAssessmentCheckoutSession/finalizeCheckoutSession) as MARKETING server actions calling those packages.
Rejected: marketing-calls-funnel-API (cross-origin CORS/cookie/iframe-Stripe friction); copy-paste rebuild (duplication/drift).

SHARED UI -> promote to @pbh/ui (shared UI only, never business logic): StepHeader, FieldError, Label, field/textarea class constants, 'Myself / Someone Else' segmented control, styled <select>.

OPEN ITEMS (non-blocking):
- Does the funnel keep /assessments + /login? If yes -> they consume @pbh/db/@pbh/linus; if funnel fully retired -> fold packages into marketing.
- Design tokens: Figma uses teal (#006e8a primary/CTA, #f5f3ee input bg, #224b60 Includes card) vs codebase Cognitive Sanctuary (navy #041632, forest #446558). Confirm whether teal is new brand primary or maps to existing tokens (see pbh-bws.39).

SUB-WORK CHECKLIST:
[ ] Extract @pbh/db, @pbh/payments, @pbh/linus from apps/funnel
[ ] Promote shared UI primitives to @pbh/ui
[ ] Build modal StepFlow state machine + 5 screens on marketing (open, transitions, terms-scroll gating, validation)
[ ] Tina landing block for 360-2502 (booking entry + Includes + $149)
[ ] Re-home 4 server actions to marketing; wire Stripe Embedded Checkout + webhook + Linus enrollment end-to-end
[ ] Reconcile design tokens
[ ] QA: test-mode payment through the modal (account -> consent row -> Stripe test card -> enrollment) + webhook backstop

RELATES TO: pbh-bws.17 (signup/consent/payment surfaces — was 'no Figma', now superseded by these designs on marketing), pbh-bws.20 (landing page), pbh-bws.21 (account creation + consent), pbh-bws.15 (component-library extension from V1), pbh-bws.24 (signed-token handoff), pbh-bws.39 (Stripe Appearance/brand tokens).
notes:
OPEN ITEMS RESOLVED (2026-07-08):
1. Funnel /assessments + /login STAY — the funnel is likely to become the authenticated app (post-purchase). So marketing = public site + booking modal flow; funnel = the app. Both consume shared packages -> extraction into @pbh/db / @pbh/payments / @pbh/linus is confirmed (do NOT fold into marketing).
2. Design tokens DEFERRED — Figma designs are not finalized; skip the teal-vs-Cognitive-Sanctuary token reconciliation for now. Build against existing @pbh/tokens; revisit once designs lock. (Drop the 'Reconcile design tokens' checklist item from scope for this pass.)
title: Booking StepFlow (5 Figma designs) — marketing-site modal, end-to-end

description:

- @pbh/db: Drizzle schema + migrations + Neon client (from apps/funnel/src/db). Keep migration ownership deliberate (CI applies on prod deploy; previews share prod DB — gate to VERCEL_ENV=production).
- @pbh/payments: Stripe server helpers + Checkout Session + webhook handler (from apps/funnel/src/lib/stripe).
- @pbh/linus: enrollment client (from apps/funnel/src/lib/linus).
Wire transpilePackages + tsconfig/eslint presets. Funnel keeps working against the new packages (no behavior change). Blocks the end-to-end wiring.

DONE. Extracted three shared workspace packages from apps/funnel (git mv, history preserved):
- @pbh/db: schema + client + audit + env + drizzle.config + migrations (migration ownership moved here; funnel/vercel.json now runs 'pnpm --filter @pbh/db run db:migrate').

close_reason: Shared @pbh/booking step components + booking flow on marketing home (Tina block). PR #13 -> staging. .4's landing block absorbed into .3.
description: Client-side modal StepFlow on the marketing site presenting the 5 Figma designs: open/close, step transitions (details -> consent -> payment -> done), focus trap + a11y, back-drop, ESC handling. Screens per Figma 360-2854 (Welcome/details), 466-245/360-3214 (Almost there! terms with scroll-to-enable gating), 360-3226 (Payment shell). Reuse funnel step markup as reference; use @pbh/ui primitives from T2. Payment screen mounts the Stripe Embedded Checkout container (deep wiring lands in T5). Build against existing @pbh/tokens (design tokens deferred — designs not finalized).
notes: DONE (PR #13 -> staging). Extracted funnel step forms into new @pbh/booking package with injected per-step action + shared types/field-options/us-states; funnel StepFlow refactored to consume them (injects its real actions; consent stays checkbox). Marketing: new bookingStepFlow Tina block replaces intakeForm in home.mdx; inline shared SignupForm opens accessible Modal (portal/focus-trap/ESC) -> shared DetailsForm -> ConsentForm -> payment shell -> done; per-step actions STUBBED (real wiring = .5). Ported animate-error-in keyframe; regenerated Tina types. Verified typecheck+lint(all) + home SSR smoke test (200).
title: Build modal StepFlow state machine + 5 screens on marketing

close_reason: Shared @pbh/booking step components + booking flow on marketing home (Tina block). PR #13 -> staging. .4's landing block absorbed into .3.

CTA 'Book Your Assessment' opens the StepFlow modal (T3) seeded with name+email. Decide reuse-vs-retire of the existing IntakeForm/HubSpot block.

close_reason: Step server actions re-homed to marketing; Stripe/webhook/Linus wired end-to-end. Webhook consolidated on the funnel (single account-scoped endpoint) and the demo funnel /get-started stepper retired. PR #13 (-> staging).
description: Re-home the 4 step server actions (createAccount, completeProfile, recordConsent, createAssessmentCheckoutSession/finalizeCheckoutSession) as MARKETING server actions calling the shared packages (T1). Wire the full end-to-end path: account creation -> consent row -> Stripe Embedded Checkout session -> finalize (verify/persist/enroll) -> Stripe webhook backstop -> Linus enrollment. Add marketing env (Stripe keys, DB URL) and the webhook route. Post-payment handoff to the funnel app (/assessments) — reuse the signed-token handoff pattern in @pbh/types where applicable.
notes: Implementation complete on branch tuily/booking-stepflow (PR #13). Extracted shared server orchestration into @pbh/booking/server (email/db-errors/consent/request-meta helpers, fulfill, register-and-enroll, action cores, checkout core, webhook handler, resolveBookingUserId auth seam). Shared PaymentStep client component. Funnel actions/webhook/PaymentStep now delegate to the package; moved funnel lib/* + fulfill.ts + register-and-enroll.ts deleted. Marketing: real step actions + payment actions + /api/stripe/webhook + deps + transpilePackages + .env.example; DoneStep hands off to funnel /login?email=. Clerk deferred behind resolveBookingUserId seam. Verified: pnpm typecheck (all pass), lint (changed files), funnel next build (green), marketing next build (green; only Tina fetch ECONNREFUSED, env-only). Live Stripe-test end-to-end deferred to pbh-ggr.6 QA.
title: Re-home step server actions to marketing + wire Stripe/webhook/Linus end-to-end

close_reason: E2E test-mode payment through the marketing modal verified: signup -> details -> consent -> Embedded Checkout -> funnel webhook recorded payment (visa 4242, $149) -> Linus register + 3 enrollments -> /login renders them. Also confirmed on the staging preview deploy.
description: Verify the whole flow in test mode: open modal from the landing block -> details -> terms (scroll gating) -> Stripe test card -> account created + consent row written + payment recorded + Linus enrollment + webhook backstop fires. Include a11y (focus trap, keyboard, WCAG) and error paths (declined card, network drop, double-submit). Relates to pbh-bws.29 (E2E Playwright) and pbh-bws.31 (WCAG/Lighthouse).
title: QA: end-to-end test-mode payment through the marketing modal

close_reason: Migrated to Checkout Sessions API (ui_mode elements) + Payment Element via CheckoutElementsProvider/checkout.confirm; verified E2E
description: Per Stripe's current recommendation, replace the raw PaymentIntent integration on tuily/stripe-payment-demo with a Checkout Session (ui_mode: elements) initialized via CheckoutElementsProvider (@stripe/react-stripe-js/checkout) and confirmed client-side with checkout.confirm. Keep the on-page Payment Element UX, Customer creation/attach, confirmation screen, and the webhook/idempotent fulfill backstop unchanged.
title: Migrate payment step from PaymentIntent to Checkout Sessions API (ui_mode elements) + Payment Element

close_reason: Implemented Linus client (register/enroll/get-report), /linus register+enroll page with campaign enrollment links, LINUS_CAMPAIGNS env config, gender/education enum alignment (funnel only), linus_participant_id column + migration 0002. Typecheck/lint/build pass; secrets server-only. Migration left for CI to apply on prod deploy per user. Full e2e test pending sandbox creds.
description: Add server-side Linus API client (OAuth2 client-credentials, register/enroll/get-report), a /linus page that registers a funnel user by email and shows campaigns + enrollment links, LINUS_CAMPAIGNS env config, align gender/educationLevel values to Linus enums (funnel only), and add linus_participant_id to users. See plan currently-we-dont-have-silly-kite.md
title: Linus Health API integration: register/enroll page + enum alignment

close_reason: Serve stored redirect for active enrollments instead of re-POSTing; corrected enrollSubject/listEnrollments docstrings per Linus feedback. typecheck+lint green.
description: Linus (Dan) confirmed re-POSTing enrollSubject is idempotent only for 'assigned' enrollments; for 'started' it mints a new enrollment id + redirect, orphaning in-progress work and the eventual report. The API exposes no per-enrollment status to distinguish the two. Fix: serve the stored redirect for active enrollments instead of re-POSTing; keep listEnrollments only to tell active from completed-pending.
title: Don't re-POST enrollSubject for active enrollments (started-state orphaning)

close_reason: Webhook consolidated on funnel (marketing route deleted); funnel /get-started tree, completeAssessmentSetup, and home CTA cleaned up. Typecheck/build/lint pass; verified funnel webhook fulfills a marketing-originated payment end-to-end.
description: Booking flow migrated to marketing. (1) Delete the duplicate marketing /api/stripe/webhook route so Stripe events are handled once, on the funnel. (2) Delete the now-dead funnel /get-started tree, the stranded completeAssessmentSetup export, and repoint the funnel home CTA at /login.
title: Consolidate Stripe webhook on funnel; delete funnel /get-started

close_reason: Payment button now redirects to /linus?email; /linus auto register+enrolls and shows links. Extracted runRegisterAndEnroll core + EnrollmentResults component. Typecheck/lint/build pass.
description: After payment (placeholder button), redirect to /linus?email and auto register+enroll the user, showing enrollment links. Refactor register-and-enroll into reusable core.
title: Wire payment step to Linus register + redirect to /linus
